mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 4.0%, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending power and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Joint strength in housing starts and retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand, which expands corporate profit margins and supports equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising HOUST and rising RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side efficiency, which lowers input cost inflation and supports equity multiples over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 5-day forward returns in > 55% of observations during early-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.5%, the cost of capital rises sufficiently to compress equity risk premiums, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral in > 60% of periods where FEDFUNDS stays above 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly proxies for corporate revenue expansion, which mechanically translates into higher earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 growth vs 5-day SPX returns remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and stable consumer price inflation indicates a goldilocks macro environment that sustains equity risk appetite, driving positive 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and CPIAUCSL between 1.5% and 2.5% YoY fails to predict positive 20-day returns in > 55% of out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-unemployment regime, housing starts act as a leading indicator for durable goods consumption and business investment, amplifying positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: HOUST growth produces negative 60-day forward returns in > 60% of instances when UNRATE is below 4.5%.
